header.description: CEO accuracy measured against correct pipeline routing, correct lead selection, correct escalation, and complete result aggregation

routing_accuracy_required.description: CEO must route to correct lead archetype based on request type and pipeline phase (per ceo.md Pipeline Routing table lines 381-393, Routing Patterns)
routing_accuracy_required.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO assessed request type and selected lead archetype matching the routing pattern table

scope_accuracy_required.description: CEO must confirm request has clear vertical slice (objective, boundaries, constraints, stop condition) before routing (per ceo.md lines 250-266, USER REQUEST EVALUATION)
scope_accuracy_required.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO verified scope completeness before dispatch

aggregation_accuracy_required.description: CEO must aggregate lead results completely without loss — all lead outputs included in CEO return (per ceo.md lines 128, 530, 572-575)
aggregation_accuracy_required.observable_from_log: Execution log shows CEO included all lead results in aggregated response, no lead results omitted

verifies_scope_clear_before_routing.description: CEO confirms request has clear objective, phase, and boundaries before routing to any lead (per ceo.md lines 250-266, Route Before You Run line 196-197)
verifies_scope_clear_before_routing.observable_from_log: Execution log shows scope verification step present before first dispatch

selects_correct_lead_based_on_task_type.description: CEO selects correct lead: SCOPER-LEAD for scoping, ARCHITECT-LEAD for architecture, BUILDER-LEAD for implementation, VERIFIER-LEAD for verification — matches task type exactly (per ceo.md lines 32-35, 381-393)
selects_correct_lead_based_on_task_type.observable_from_log: Execution log shows lead archetype matches task type in every dispatch

confirms_write_boundary_before_implement.description: CEO confirms write boundary is explicitly declared before routing to builder-lead (per ceo.md DELEGATION MODEL, builder-lead dispatch contract requires Approved Architecture Brief and explicit write boundary)
confirms_write_boundary_before_implement.observable_from_log: Execution log shows write boundary field present in builder-lead dispatch brief

does_not_route_to_incorrect_lead.description: CEO does not route to incorrect lead archetype (e.g., scoping to builder, verification to scoper) (per ceo.md lines 381-393, Pipeline Routing patterns)
does_not_route_to_incorrect_lead.observable_from_log: YES if no routing mismatches; NO if task routed to wrong lead archetype

does_not_route_without_clear_scope.description: CEO does not route to leads when scope is unclear — returns clarification first (per ceo.md lines 250-266, Evaluating Uncertainties lines 291-309)
does_not_route_without_clear_scope.observable_from_log: YES if clarification requested before routing when scope unclear; NO if routing proceeded without scope clarity

does_not_lose_lead_results_in_aggregatio.description: CEO does not omit, summarize away, or soften lead results in aggregated response (per ceo.md lines 530, 572-575, aggregation is synthesis not distortion)
does_not_lose_lead_results_in_aggregatio.observable_from_log: YES if all lead results present and unfiltered in CEO aggregated response; NO if lead results omitted or softened
